params.name: customer
params.description: Only return credit notes for the customer specified by this customer ID.

params.name: ending_before
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `ending_before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with `obj_bar`, your subsequent call can include `ending_before=obj_bar` in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: invoice
params.description: Only return credit notes for the invoice specified by this invoice ID.

params.name: limit
params.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 10.

params.name: starting_after
params.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `starting_after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with `obj_foo`, your subsequent call can include `starting_after=obj_foo` in order to fetch the next page of the list.

items.docs:
Issue a credit note to adjust the amount of a finalized invoice. For a status=open invoice, a credit note reduces its amount_due. For a status=paid invoice, a credit note does not affect its amount_due. Instead, it can result in any combination of the following:

  • Refund: create a new refund (using refund_amount) or link an existing refund (using refund).
  • Customer balance credit: credit the cust

params.name: amount
params.description: The integer amount in cents (or local equivalent) representing the total amount of the credit note.

params.name: credit_amount
params.description: The integer amount in cents (or local equivalent) representing the amount to credit the customer's balance, which will be automatically applied to their next invoice.

params.name: effective_at
params.description: The date when this credit note is in effect. Same as `created` unless overwritten. When defined, this value replaces the system-generated 'Date of issue' printed on the credit note PDF.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: invoice
params.description: ID of the invoice.

params.name: lines
params.description: Line items that make up the credit note.

params.name: memo
params.description: The credit note's memo appears on the credit note PDF.

params.name: metadata
params.description: Set of [key-value pairs](https://stripe.com/docs/api/metadata) that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format. Individual keys can be unset by posting an empty value to them. All keys can be unset by posting an empty value to `metadata`.

params.name: out_of_band_amount
params.description: The integer amount in cents (or local equivalent) representing the amount that is credited outside of Stripe.

params.name: reason
params.description: Reason for issuing this credit note, one of `duplicate`, `fraudulent`, `order_change`, or `product_unsatisfactory`

params.name: refund
params.description: ID of an existing refund to link this credit note to.

params.name: refund_amount
params.description: The integer amount in cents (or local equivalent) representing the amount to refund. If set, a refund will be created for the charge associated with the invoice.

params.name: shipping_cost
params.description: When shipping_cost contains the shipping_rate from the invoice, the shipping_cost is included in the credit note.

items.docs: Get a preview of a credit note without creating it.

items.docs: When retrieving a credit note preview, you’ll get a lines property containing the first handful of those items. This URL you can retrieve the full (paginated) list of line items.

items.docs: Marks a credit note as void. Learn more about voiding credit notes.
